base.description: Base token values shared across all variants.

actionColors.description:
Action colors are used **exclusively** for styling Buttons borders and backgrounds.
They define interaction states for primary, secondary, subtle, and destructive buttons.

interactiveColors.description:
Interactive colors should be used **exclusively** for styling Data Entry components: Input, Select, etc.

"Overlay" colors are intended to be placed over the default background color, such as `interactiveColorAccent`.
If the default background color is not transparent, the `blend` mixin can be used to create a new color that combines the two.

neutralColors.description:
Neutral colors should be used sparingly. Prefer to use more semantic tokens for backgrounds and borders.
These colors must not be used for content as they will not pass the required contrast ratio (instead, use [Content colors](#Content Colors)).

backgroundColors.description: Use background colors for page backgrounds and containers such as cards, modals, panels or popups.

contentColors.description: Use content colors to create visual hierarchy, convey status or importance for plain text content.

notificationColors.description:
Use notifications colors to communicate messages and guidance directly to users.
For example in Message Bars to set tone and urgency in a conversational context.

Weak variants do not meet graphical or text contrast and should only be used decoratively.

severityColors.description:
Use severity colors for icons, indicators, and visual signals to help users quickly scan for criticality, risk, or state without relying only on text.
Severity colors should be used to aid identifying the semantic meaning of an element; not used to communicate tone.

syntaxColors.description:
These colors should be used for syntax highlighting in programming languages.
The colors chosen for tokens depend on the language, its features, and aesthetics.
Prefer to use the `Code` component rather than these tokens directly as it does this mapping automatically.
See [Content Colors](#Content Colors)

borderColors.description: Border color should be used to outline or divide content and UI elements.

spacing.description:
Use these tokens for adding margin between elements or padding within containers.

Just because a spacing token is equal to a pixel value you may need, it does not mean it's appropriate to
use the spacing variables.

zIndexes.description:
Used to position elements in the z-axis.
If a variable does not suit your purpose, set a value relatively, such as `zindexModal +1`.

lineLength.description:
Use to improve readability and [accessibility](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/visual-presentation.html)
in blocks of text by preventing overly long lines.

lineLengthMinimum.description: Minimum line length for optimal readability.

actionColorBackgroundDestructiveSecondar.$description: Default background for Destructive Secondary Buttons.
Disabled background for Destructive Secondary Buttons.

black.$description: Alternate background color in dark color schemes and inverted background in light color schemes.

interactiveColorBackground.$description: Default background color for Data entry components.

interactiveColorBackgroundDisabled.$description: Background color for disabled Data entry components.

lineHeightNormal.$description: Default for body text; comfortable, standard line height same as `lineHeight`

lineHeightSingle.$description:
Very tight. Use where space is extremely limited and text is not expected to wrap.
Ideal for buttons, badges, input fields, and compact labels.

lineHeightSnug.$description: Compact but a bit more readable

lineHeightTight.$description: Slightly more space than single, still compact

lineLength.$description:
Use to improve readability and [accessibility](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/visual-presentation.html)
in blocks of text by preventing overly long lines.

lineLengthMinimum.$description: Minimum line length for optimal readability.

white.$description: Alternate background color in light color schemes and inverted background in dark color schemes.

family-colorScheme.description: Family and color scheme overrides grouped by family-colorScheme combinations.

backgroundColorPopup.$description: Default background color for popovers and tooltips.

backgroundColorScrim.$description: Used for page overlays under modals or when masking UI.

borderActiveColor.$deprecated: Use `interactiveColorBorderActive` for Data Entry components or `actionColorBorderSecondaryActive` for Buttons.

borderColor.$description: Default border color for elements that meets 3:1 graphical contrast (e.g., menu dividers).

borderColorStrong.$description: Border color with stronger visual emphasis that meets 3:1 graphical contrast.

borderColorWeak.$description: Decorative-only border color that does not meet 3:1 graphical contrast.

contentColorAccentStrong.$description: Brighter brand accent text color for emphasis.

contentColorAccentWeak.$description: Brand accent text color that does not pass 4.5:1 contrast ratio.

contentColorActive.$description: Text color for headings, titles, and prominent text content.

contentColorDefault.$description: Text color for paragraphs and standard content.

contentColorDisabled.$description: Text color for disabled content that does not pass 4.5:1 contrast.

contentColorInverted.$description: Text color used on top of inverted backgrounds.

contentColorLinkVisitedDisabled.$description: Text color for visited links in a disabled state.

contentColorMuted.$description: Subdued text color for less prominent content (use sparingly).

contentColorNegative.$description: Text color for error or negative status messaging.

contentColorNegativeWeak.$description: Disabled negative text color that does not pass 4.5:1 contrast.

focusColor.$description: Visual focus ring color used for focus box-shadow. Should not be directly applied to UI elements.
